items.why: Students confuse andante (walking, moderate) with adagio (slow). Andante literally means 'going/walking' in Italian - it's a comfortable pace, not slow.

items.q: What does 'piano' (p) mean as a dynamic marking?
items.why: Students see 'piano' and think of the instrument. As dynamic, p = soft (from Italian). The instrument is actually a shortened 'pianoforte' (soft-loud) because it could play at varying dynamics, unlike the harpsichord.

items.q: How many crotchets are in a semibreve?
items.why: Students confuse the relative durations. A semibreve (whole note) = 2 minims = 4 crotchets = 8 quavers. Each level halves in length.

items.q: Which tempo marking is the slowest?
items.why: Correct answer: Adagio. “Allegro” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: A trill is best described as:
items.correct: Rapid alternation between two adjacent notes
items.why: It's Rapid alternation between two adjacent notes. “A sudden loud accent” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Approximately how many beats per minute is a typical Allegro tempo?
items.why: The real figure is 120. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
